label: MongoDB Atlas
description: Connect to a MongoDB Atlas cluster using a connection string to query and manage your cloud-hosted MongoDB databases.

form.title: Connect MongoDB Atlas
form.subtitle: Paste your Atlas connection string to let Anton query your MongoDB cloud cluster.

methods.label: Connection String
methods.description: Paste the mongodb+srv:// URI from Atlas Console. The fastest way to connect — no extra credentials needed beyond what's already embedded in the string.
methods.how_to:
## Get your MongoDB Atlas connection string

1. Log in to [MongoDB Atlas](https://cloud.mongodb.com) and open your project.
2. In the left sidebar, click **Database** under the *Deployment* section.
3. Find your cluster and click the **Connect** button.
4. Choose **Connect your application**.
5. Select **Driver: Python** (or any driver — the URI format is the same) and copy the connection string. It looks like:
   `mongodb+srv://<username>:<password>@cluster0.xxxxx.mongodb.net/?retryWrites=true&w=majority`
6. Replace `<password>` with your database user's password before pasting.
7. Paste the full string into the **Connection String** field below.

## Allow your IP address

1. In Atlas, go to **Security → Network Access**.
2. Click **Add IP Address**.
3. Add your current IP or use `0.0.0.0/0` to allow all IPs (not recommended for production).
4. Click **Confirm**.

## Notes

- The database user is separate from your Atlas account login. Create one under **Security → Database Access** if needed.
- Make sure the user has at least **Read** privileges on the target database.
- If you omit the database name from the URI, Atlas connects to the `admin` database by default. Append `/<dbname>` before the `?` to target a specific database.

fields.name: connection_string
fields.label: Connection String
fields.description: Full mongodb+srv:// URI including credentials. Found in Atlas Console → Database → Connect → Connect your application.

fields.name: database_name
fields.label: Default Database
fields.description: Optional. The database Anton will use by default. If not set, the database specified in the connection string (or 'admin') is used.

fields.name: tls_enabled
fields.label: Require TLS
fields.description: Atlas clusters enforce TLS by default. Disable only if you have explicitly turned off TLS on the cluster (not recommended).

methods.label: Username & Password
methods.description: Supply the Atlas cluster hostname, database user credentials, and database name separately instead of as a single URI.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with username and password

1. Log in to [MongoDB Atlas](https://cloud.mongodb.com) and open your project.
2. In the left sidebar, click **Database** under the *Deployment* section.
3. Find your cluster and click the **Connect** button.
4. Choose **Connect your application** and note the **hostname** portion of the URI (e.g. `cluster0.xxxxx.mongodb.net`).
5. If you don't have a database user, go to **Security → Database Access → Add New Database User**. Create a user with a strong password and assign appropriate roles.
6. Go to **Security → Network Access** and add your IP address so Atlas allows the connection.
7. Enter the hostname, username, password, and target database name in the fields below.

## Notes

- The port for Atlas clusters is always `27017`.
- Atlas enforces TLS — do not disable it unless your cluster configuration explicitly allows plaintext connections.
- Assign the least-privileged role that meets your needs (e.g. `readAnyDatabase` for read-only access).

fields.name: host
fields.label: Cluster Hostname
fields.description: The SRV hostname for your Atlas cluster, without the mongodb+srv:// prefix or port.

fields.name: username
fields.label: Database Username
fields.description: The MongoDB database user created under Atlas → Security → Database Access.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Database Password
fields.description: Password for the database user above. This is NOT your Atlas account password.

fields.name: database_name
fields.label: Database Name
fields.description: Target database to connect to. Leave blank to connect to the 'admin' database.

fields.name: tls_enabled
fields.label: Require TLS
fields.description: Atlas clusters enforce TLS by default. Keep this enabled unless explicitly disabled on your cluster.
